statements.status_notes: Review by 2026-07-29 (6 weeks after 2026-06-17): re-check whether the next prometheus-operator release has dropped the legacy github.com/docker/docker dependency and the OpenTelemetry Operator has bumped to it; retire this statement once the dependency is gone.
statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-34040 is a Docker Engine AuthZ-plugin bypass in the Moby server: a server-side request handler accepts an oversized request body that the AuthZ plugin then fails to evaluate. The quay.io/stackstate/opentelemetry-target-allocator image ships only the targetallocator Go binary (images/opentelemetry-target-allocator/Dockerfile in StackVista/docker-images), built from ./cmd/otel-allocator in open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator. github.com/docker/docker enters the binary transitively through github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery; the target allocator and the operator only link the client-side packages of github.com/docker/docker and do not contain the Moby server (cmd/dockerd, daemon, api/server), so the vulnerable AuthZ-plugin request path is not present in the execute path. This is confirmed upstream in open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator#4926: "github.com/docker/docker is a transitive dependency pulled in by the github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery package. It only uses the client side of the docker package, whereas the vulnerabilities affect the server side." The fix lives at the new github.com/moby/moby/v2 module path (Docker Engine 29.x / v2.0.0-beta.14); Prometheus has migrated in prometheus/prometheus#18433, and once the next prometheus-operator release picks up that Prometheus version and the OpenTelemetry Operator bumps to it, the docker/docker dependency will disappear from the target allocator entirely and this VEX will become moot.

statements.status_notes: Review by 2026-07-29 (6 weeks after 2026-06-17): re-check whether the next prometheus-operator release has dropped the legacy github.com/docker/docker dependency and the OpenTelemetry Operator has bumped to it; retire this statement once the dependency is gone.
statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-41567 is a PATH-resolution flaw in the Moby server's docker cp implementation: when the daemon decompresses an archive into a target container it resolves xz/unpigz/gzip helper binaries via the container filesystem's PATH while running as host root, letting a malicious image trojanise the helper. The vulnerable code lives in the Docker Engine server (daemon/archive.go and friends). The quay.io/stackstate/opentelemetry-target-allocator image ships only the targetallocator Go binary; it contains no dockerd, no archive-extraction code path, and does not invoke docker cp on behalf of the user. github.com/docker/docker is pulled in transitively through github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery, and per upstream open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator#4926 only the client-side packages are used: "It only uses the client side of the docker package, whereas the vulnerabilities affect the server side." The fix lives at the new github.com/moby/moby/v2 module path (Docker Engine 29.x / v2.0.0-beta.14); Prometheus has migrated in prometheus/prometheus#18433, and once the next prometheus-operator release picks up that Prometheus version and the OpenTelemetry Operator bumps to it, the docker/docker dependency will disappear from the target allocator entirely and this VEX will become moot.

statements.status_notes: Review by 2026-07-29 (6 weeks after 2026-06-17): re-check whether the next prometheus-operator release has dropped the legacy github.com/docker/docker dependency and the OpenTelemetry Operator has bumped to it; retire this statement once the dependency is gone.
statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-42306 is a TOCTOU race in the Moby server's docker cp bind-mount handling: a container process can redirect a bind mount to an arbitrary host path between the daemon's path resolution and its mount syscall. The vulnerable code lives in the Docker Engine server (daemon/cluster/executor and the cp/mount path). The quay.io/stackstate/opentelemetry-target-allocator image ships only the targetallocator Go binary; it contains no dockerd, performs no bind-mount setup, and is not invoked as a Docker daemon. github.com/docker/docker is pulled in transitively through github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery, and per upstream open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator#4926 only the client-side packages are used: "It only uses the client side of the docker package, whereas the vulnerabilities affect the server side." The fix lives at the new github.com/moby/moby/v2 module path (Docker Engine 29.x / v2.0.0-beta.14); Prometheus has migrated in prometheus/prometheus#18433, and once the next prometheus-operator release picks up that Prometheus version and the OpenTelemetry Operator bumps to it, the docker/docker dependency will disappear from the target allocator entirely and this VEX will become moot.

statements.status_notes: Review by 2026-07-29 (6 weeks after 2026-06-17): re-check whether the next prometheus-operator release has dropped the legacy github.com/docker/docker dependency and the OpenTelemetry Operator has bumped to it; retire this statement once the dependency is gone.
statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-33997 is an off-by-one error in the Moby server's plugin privilege validation during docker plugin install: the daemon's privilege-set comparison can accept a privilege set that differs from the one approved by the user, and plugins requesting exactly one privilege are not compared at all. The vulnerable code lives in the Docker Engine server (the plugin install/privilege validation path in the daemon). The quay.io/stackstate/opentelemetry-target-allocator image ships only the targetallocator Go binary; it contains no dockerd, executes no docker plugin install flow, and is not invoked as a Docker daemon. github.com/docker/docker is pulled in transitively through github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery, and per upstream open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator#4926 only the client-side packages are used: "It only uses the client side of the docker package, whereas the vulnerabilities affect the server side." The fix lives at the new github.com/moby/moby/v2 module path (Docker Engine 29.3.1 / v2.0.0-beta.8); Prometheus has migrated in prometheus/prometheus#18433, and once the next prometheus-operator release picks up that Prometheus version and the OpenTelemetry Operator bumps to it, the docker/docker dependency will disappear from the target allocator entirely and this VEX will become moot.

statements.status_notes: Review by 2026-07-29 (6 weeks after 2026-06-17): re-check whether the next prometheus-operator release has dropped the legacy github.com/docker/docker dependency and the OpenTelemetry Operator has bumped to it; retire this statement once the dependency is gone.
statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-41568 is a TOCTOU symlink race in the Moby server's docker cp mountpoint setup: between GetResourcePath resolving the in-container destination and createIfNotExists materialising it via os.MkdirAll/os.OpenFile, a container process can swap a path component for a symlink, causing the daemon (running as host root) to create an empty file or directory at an arbitrary absolute host path. The vulnerable code lives in the Docker Engine server (daemon/archive.go and the docker cp mountpoint setup path), classified as CWE-61 / CWE-367. The quay.io/stackstate/opentelemetry-target-allocator image ships only the targetallocator Go binary; it contains no dockerd, performs no docker cp mountpoint setup, and is not invoked as a Docker daemon. github.com/docker/docker is pulled in transitively through github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery, and per upstream open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator#4926 only the client-side packages are used: "It only uses the client side of the docker package, whereas the vulnerabilities affect the server side." The fix lives at the new github.com/moby/moby/v2 module path (Docker Engine 29.5.1 / v2.0.0-beta.14); Prometheus has migrated in prometheus/prometheus#18433, and once the next prometheus-operator release picks up that Prometheus version and the OpenTelemetry Operator bumps to it, the docker/docker dependency will disappear from the target allocator entirely and this VEX will become moot.
